event: The Virgin and Child in a jasmine bower
symbols: jasmine flowers — purity, virginity, and the sweetness of grace
red cherries — the blood of the Passion and the fruits of redemption
enclosed garden of foliage — the hortus conclusus, Mary's perpetual virginity (Song 4:12)
Christ Child looking upward — awareness of His divine mission beyond earthly intimacy
Mary's downcast gaze — contemplative sorrow and maternal tenderness
description: A young woman with long auburn hair, wearing a blue robe over a white garment with a red sash, sits cradling a nude infant on her lap. Dense green foliage with small white jasmine flowers fills the background, forming a natural bower around them. The child sits upright, looking up toward the viewer, while the mother gazes down tenderly. In the foreground, her hand reaches toward a branch bearing small red cherries. The scene is intimate and enclosed, with the vegetation creating a private, garden-like space.
theological_description: The Madonna in a jasmine bower draws upon the Song of Songs imagery of the hortus conclusus — 'A garden locked is my sister, my bride, a spring locked, a fountain sealed' (SNG.4.12). The enclosed garden of fragrant flowers signifies Mary's virginal purity and her unique role as the vessel of the Incarnation. The jasmine, with its white blossoms, traditionally symbolizes purity and the grace of the Holy Spirit. The red cherries toward which Mary reaches foreshadow the blood of the Passion — even in this scene of maternal tenderness, the shadow of the Cross is present. The Christ Child's upward gaze suggests His awareness of the divine mission, while Mary's contemplative expression recalls Luke's note that she 'treasured all these things in her heart' (LUK.2.19). The lush vegetation evokes Eden restored — through the new Eve and the new Adam, paradise is reopened to humanity.

scripture_refs.reason: The enclosed garden imagery applied to Mary's virginity, evoked by the bower of jasmine
Mary's contemplative gaze reflects her pondering these things in her heart
The Incarnation — the Holy Spirit overshadowing Mary, resulting in the Christ Child she holds
The virgin shall conceive and bear a son — fulfilled in the Madonna and Child

_meta.title: Madonna and Child in a Jasmine Bower
